title: Finding Files
content:
### find — Search the Filesystem

The most powerful search tool.

**By name:**

```bash
find /home -name "*.txt"
find . -iname "README*"     # case-insensitive
find . -name "[a-z]*.py"    # glob pattern
```

**By type:**

```bash
find . -type f               # regular files only
find . -type d               # directories
find . -type l               # symlinks
```

**By size and time:**

```bash
find . -size +100M           # files larger than 100MB
find . -size -1k             # files smaller than 1KB
find . -mtime -7             # modified in last 7 days
find . -mmin +60             # modified more than 60 min ago
```

**Executing commands on results:**

```bash
find . -name "*.log" -exec rm {} \;
find . -type f -exec chmod 644 {} +
```

### locate / updatedb

Fast database-backed search (requires `mlocate` or `plocate`):

```bash
sudo updatedb           # update database
locate .bashrc          # instant search
```

### which — Locate a Command

```bash
which python3           # /usr/bin/python3
which -a ls             # show all matches in PATH
```

### whereis — Find Binary, Source, and Man Page

```bash
whereis ls              # /usr/bin/ls /usr/share/man/man1/ls.1.gz
```

### type — How Shell Interprets a Command

```bash
type ls                 # ls is aliased to 'ls --color=auto'
type cd                 # cd is a shell builtin
type /bin/ls            # /bin/ls is /bin/ls
```

### fd — Modern Alternative to find

```bash
fd "pattern"            # fast, user-friendly
fd -e py                # all .py files
```
codeExample:
find /home -name "*.txt" -type f
find . -size +1M -exec ls -lh {} \;
locate bashrc
which python3
type cd
fd -e pdf

quiz.question: How do you find all files modified in the last 7 days?
What does `which python3` do?
options: Shows the path to the python3 executable
Shows the man page for python3
